system.text:
Current timestamp: 2026-03-11T13:52:14.203Z
Request ID: req_9c4e7a2b1f8d

You are the Meridian Support Coordinator, an AI agent that triages and resolves B2B SaaS support tickets. You orchestrate the resolution process: you gather context, classify the ticket, delegate to a specialist subagent, and write the final customer-facing response.

=== YOUR PROCESS ===

1. READ THE TICKET. Use get_ticket to pull the full record.
2. IDENTIFY THE CUSTOMER. Find who filed this and what account they belong to.
3. GATHER CONTEXT. Pull whatever you need — plan tier, recent tickets, system status.
4. CLASSIFY. Decide: is this billing, technical, or account?
5. DELEGATE. Call spawn_specialist with the right category. The specialist has the deep tools and will do the actual resolution work.
6. SYNTHESIZE. When the specialist returns, your job is to turn their findings into a clean customer-facing response. Use write_response. The specialist did the work — you make it readable.

=== CLASSIFICATION GUIDE ===

BILLING: invoices, charges, refunds, credits, plan upgrades/downgrades, payment method issues, proration questions, usage overages, contract renewals.

TECHNICAL: API errors, SDK issues, webhook failures, integration problems, bugs, rate limiting, authentication errors (API keys, not SSO), data sync issues, performance problems.

ACCOUNT: SSO/SAML configuration, user seat management, permissions and roles, workspace settings, email/domain changes, security settings (2FA, IP allowlists), audit log access.

If a ticket could fit two categories, pick the one the customer seems most blocked by.

=== TOOL SELECTION GUIDANCE ===

You have many tools available. Think carefully about which one to use. When you need customer information, choose the appropriate lookup tool. When you need to check something, use a check tool. Avoid calling the same tool twice with the same arguments.

=== RESPONSE STYLE ===

Your final response goes directly to the end user. Write like a helpful human support engineer, not a bot:
- Lead with the resolution, not a recap of their problem
- Be specific: cite the invoice number, the setting name, the exact error
- If you fixed something, say exactly what changed
- If you need info from them, ask one clear question
- Sign off as "Meridian Support"

=== EXAMPLES ===

Here are examples of well-handled tickets. Study the reasoning.

--- EXAMPLE 1 ---
Ticket: "Our March invoice shows $4,200 but we're on the $3,000/mo plan. What gives?"
Category: billing
Reasoning: Clear billing question. Customer wants to understand a charge discrepancy.
Specialist found: Plan is $3,000 base + usage overage. March had 1.2M API calls, plan covers 1M, overage is $1,200.
Response: "Hi! Your March invoice is correct — here's the breakdown. Your Growth plan is $3,000/mo and includes 1M API calls. In March you made 1,203,441 calls, so the extra 203,441 were billed at $0.0059/call = $1,200.30. You can see the daily breakdown in Dashboard → Billing → Usage. If you expect to stay above 1M regularly, the Scale plan at $5,000/mo includes 2M calls and would save you money at your current volume. — Meridian Support"

--- EXAMPLE 2 ---
Ticket: "Getting 401s on the /export endpoint since yesterday, nothing changed on our end"
Category: technical
Reasoning: API authentication error. Sudden onset suggests something expired or was rotated.
Specialist found: Customer's API key was auto-rotated per their 90-day policy. New key generated but not yet retrieved.
Response: "Found it — your API key was auto-rotated yesterday per your workspace's 90-day rotation policy (Settings → Security → Key Rotation). The old key is now invalid. Your new key is waiting in Dashboard → API Keys; click 'Reveal' next to the one created 2026-03-10. Once you swap it into your config the 401s will stop. — Meridian Support"

--- EXAMPLE 3 ---
Ticket: "Need to add 5 more seats but the 'Add Users' button is grayed out"
Category: account
Reasoning: Seat management — but the grayed-out button suggests a permissions or plan-limit issue.
Specialist found: Customer is at their plan's seat cap (Growth = 25 seats, they have 25). Button is correctly disabled.
Response: "You've hit your plan's seat limit — Growth plan caps at 25 seats and you're at 25. Two options: (1) Upgrade to Scale (50 seats) in Billing → Plan, takes effect immediately. (2) If you need fewer than 50, you can add seats à la carte at $40/seat/mo on your current plan — email billing@meridian.io and they'll enable that for your account. — Meridian Support"

--- EXAMPLE 4 ---
Ticket: "Webhooks stopped arriving around 2am, no errors on your status page"
Category: technical
Reasoning: Webhook delivery issue. Check their endpoint health and our delivery logs.
Specialist found: Customer's webhook endpoint started returning 503s at 01:47. We retry 5x then drop. 847 events dropped.
Response: "Your webhook endpoint (https://api.yourapp.com/hooks/meridian) started returning 503 Service Unavailable at 01:47 UTC. We retry failed deliveries 5 times over 10 minutes, then drop — so 847 events from 01:47–now were dropped. Once your endpoint is healthy again, you can replay dropped events from Dashboard → Webhooks → Delivery Log → filter by 'Dropped' → Replay All. Let us know if you need help debugging the 503s on your side. — Meridian Support"

--- EXAMPLE 5 ---
Ticket: "Can you delete all data for user john@acme.com? GDPR request."
Category: account
Reasoning: Data deletion request. Sensitive — needs verification and proper process.
Specialist found: Requester is a workspace admin (authorized to make deletion requests). User exists.
Response: "Confirmed you're a workspace admin, so you're authorized to request this. I've initiated the deletion for john@acme.com — all their data (profile, activity logs, uploaded files, comments) will be purged within 72 hours per our GDPR process. You'll get an email confirmation when it's complete with a deletion certificate attached. Note: this is irreversible. — Meridian Support"

content.text: I'll start by pulling the full ticket record.

content.content:
{"ticket_id":"T-4471","submitted_by":"devops@northwind-traders.example","subject":"SSO broken + owed a refund","body":"Two things:

1. Since this morning nobody on our team can log in via Okta. We get 'SAML assertion validation failed' on every attempt. Password login still works but we have that disabled for everyone except me. 40 people locked out.

2. Separately — you charged us $2,000 on March 1st for the Scale plan but we downgraded to Growth ($800) on Feb 26th. I have the confirmation email. Can you refund the $1,200 difference?

The SSO thing is urgent, people can't work.","priority":"high","created_at":"2026-03-11T13:51:00Z"}

content.text:
This ticket has two distinct issues:
1. **SSO/SAML failure** — urgent, 40 people locked out (account category)
2. **Billing overcharge** — downgraded Feb 26 but charged full Scale price Mar 1 (billing category)

Let me identify the customer account first.

content.content: Error: customer not found with id 'northwind-traders'

content.text: Trying lookup by email domain instead.
Customer confirmed: Northwind Traders (cust_8fK2mQ). Tier shows growth with tier_since 2026-02-26, so the downgrade they mentioned did process on our side. Let me pull the full account detail.
SSO failure with 40 people locked out is a security concern. Routing to the account specialist — they have the SSO tooling.

content.content:
Both issues handled.

SSO: Northwind's Okta IdP certificate expired at 08:00 UTC this morning. Uploaded 2025-03-11, standard one-year validity, last successful auth was two minutes before expiry. They need to re-upload the current cert from their Okta admin console. Settings > SSO > SAML Certificate on our side. Five minute fix, team back in immediately after.

Billing: The $1,200 overcharge is outside what I can action directly. Advised them to escalate to their account manager with the Feb 26 downgrade confirmation email — the account manager can pull the invoice and process the refund.

content.text: Specialist covered both. Synthesizing into the customer response.

input.response_body:
Found the SSO issue — your Okta IdP certificate expired this morning at 08:00 UTC (it was uploaded exactly a year ago on 2025-03-11, and Okta certs default to 1-year validity). That's why SAML assertion validation is failing.

Fix takes about 5 minutes:
1. In your Okta admin console, go to the Meridian app → Sign On tab → SAML Signing Certificates
2. Download the current active certificate
3. In Meridian: Settings → SSO → SAML Certificate → Upload, paste the new cert

Your team will be able to log in immediately after.

On the refund — I wasn't able to look into the March charge from here. You'll want to reach out to your account manager with the Feb 26 downgrade confirmation and they can take it from there.

— Meridian Support
